statement.en: DUB regulators of YAP/TAZ activity in mesothelioma and head and neck cancer
statement.summary: The Hippo pathway is evolutionarily conserved to regulate multiple biological processes including development, organ size, apoptosis, and tissue regeneration, thereby maintaining tissue homeostasis. This tumour-suppressive signalling cascade acts inactivates the downstream effectors and oncogenes YAP/TAZ, preventing their binding to the TEAD transcription factors. Disruption of the Hippo pathway and aberrant YAP/TAZ activity are observed in many cancers such as pleural mesothelioma (PM) and head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC), where there are known genetic drivers. Deubiquitinating enzymes (DUBs) have been identified as direct or indirect regulators of the pathway. However, questions remain unresolved, notably around the specificity of their interaction with the Hippo components

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents provisional findings on DUB regulation of YAP/TAZ in cancer contexts. The abstract explicitly identifies unresolved questions ('questions remain unresolved') and incomplete mechanistic understanding ('specificity of their interaction...unclear'). This is consistent with active research under peer review (preprint category, 2026 date suggests recent submission). The claims are empirically testable but not yet validated across full literature consensus.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: DUB酵素が脱ユビキチン化の逆数を握ることで、YAP/TAZというオンコジーンの怪物が目覚める。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 上の王（YAP/TAZ）が、下の二つの目（DUB活性の領域）から鎖が解ける時、門番TEADを超えて暴走する。
